componentDescription: Modal is a dialog overlay that captures user focus for important interactions — confirmations, forms, detail views, and multi-step flows. It blocks the underlying page and must be explicitly dismissed. For mobile-friendly layouts, use useDrawerOnMobile to automatically switch to a Drawer on small screens.
features: Controlled open/close state via isOpen and onClose
Standard header (title + subtitle) and footer (primaryAction + secondaryAction) with show/hide toggles
Fully custom header and footer slots for non-standard layouts
headerRightSlot for adding actions or badges in the header without replacing it entirely
Optional divider between header and body content
Responsive: switches to bottom-sheet Drawer on mobile when useDrawerOnMobile is true
Skeleton loading mode while content is being fetched
Width control via minWidth, maxWidth; height via maxHeight
Backdrop click to close (enabled by default, disable with closeOnBackdropClick=false)
compositionPattern: Pass content as children between the Modal tags. For simple confirmation dialogs, use title + primaryAction + secondaryAction props. For complex forms or custom layouts, use isCustom=true with customHeader and customFooter slots.

usageExamples.title: Confirmation dialog
usageExamples.description: Simple modal asking the user to confirm a destructive action.
usageExamples.code:
setIsOpen(false)} title="Delete project" subtitle="This action cannot be undone. All project data will be permanently removed." primaryAction={{ label: 'Delete', onClick: handleDelete }} secondaryAction={{ label: 'Cancel', onClick: () => setIsOpen(false) }} closeOnBackdropClick={false} >

Are you sure you want to delete {projectName}?

usageExamples.title: Form modal with custom footer
usageExamples.description: Modal containing a form with a custom footer for more control over button layout.
usageExamples.code: setIsOpen(false)} title="Edit profile" showFooter={false} maxWidth={600} useDrawerOnMobile >

usageExamples.title: Fully custom modal
usageExamples.description: Custom modal with isCustom=true for a non-standard layout like an image viewer.
usageExamples.code:
setIsOpen(false)} isCustom maxWidth={900} maxHeight={700} >

{imageTitle}

{imageDescription}

isOpen.llmContext: Required. Controls whether the modal is visible. Always manage this with useState. Set to true to open, false to close. The modal renders as a portal, so state should live in the parent component.

onClose.llmContext: Required. Called when the user clicks the close button or presses Escape. Set isOpen to false inside this handler. If you need to prevent accidental dismissal (e.g., unsaved form), also set closeOnBackdropClick=false and handle confirmation logic here.
onClose.propDescription: Required. Callback fired on close button click or Escape key

isCustom.llmContext: Set to true to suppress the default header/footer rendering entirely and take full control of the modal content via children. Use for non-standard layouts like image galleries, multi-panel views, or fully custom forms. When isCustom is true, title, subtitle, primaryAction, and secondaryAction props are ignored.
isCustom.propDescription: Disables default header/footer; full content control via children

useDrawerOnMobile.llmContext: Set to true for modals that appear in mobile contexts. The modal automatically renders as a bottom-sheet Drawer on small screens, providing a better mobile UX without any extra logic.
useDrawerOnMobile.propDescription: Renders as a Drawer (bottom sheet) on mobile screen sizes

closeOnBackdropClick.llmContext: Default is true. Set to false for modals with forms or multi-step flows where accidental backdrop clicks would lose user data. Always disable for checkout flows, complex forms, and irreversible confirmations.
closeOnBackdropClick.propDescription: Whether clicking the backdrop closes the modal

headerRightSlot.llmContext: Accepts a React node rendered on the right side of the modal header, next to the close button. Use for status badges, step indicators, or contextual actions without replacing the full header.
headerRightSlot.propDescription: React node rendered in the top-right of the header

skeleton.llmContext: Set to true while the modal's content data is being fetched. Displays a skeleton loading state instead of children. Use this to avoid showing an empty or partially-loaded modal.
skeleton.propDescription: Shows skeleton loading state instead of children

showDivider.llmContext: Set to true to add a visible horizontal rule between the modal header and body. Useful when the modal header and body content are visually similar and need separation.
showDivider.propDescription: Shows a divider line between header and body

primaryAction.llmContext: Pass { label: string, onClick: () => void, loading?: boolean, disabled?: boolean } for the main footer action. Rendered as a PRIMARY button. If using a form inside the modal, prefer handling submission directly in children for more control over loading/disabled states.
primaryAction.propDescription: Primary action button in the modal footer

secondaryAction.llmContext: Pass { label: string, onClick: () => void } for the footer's secondary action. Typically 'Cancel' or 'Back'. Rendered as a SECONDARY button.
secondaryAction.propDescription: Secondary action button in the modal footer
